name: Imagery
description: Art direction for photography across the brand: subject categories, editing/colour rules, and explicit exclusions. This is the deepest section in the guideline — a real photographic direction rather than a stock-photo disclaimer.
keyRule: Photography should read as authentic and operational — real vehicles, real drivers, real operations contexts — rather than generic stock imagery of unrelated business/lifestyle scenes.

pages.name: Art direction
pages.description: Overview statement establishing the intended tone: authentic, operational, on-the-ground — not staged corporate stock photography.

pages.name: People
pages.description: Guidance on depicting drivers/operators — natural poses and real work contexts over posed stock-photo people.

pages.name: Vehicles
pages.description: Rules for vehicle photography — fleet vehicles shown in real operating contexts (roads, yards), not showroom-style shots.

pages.name: Platform
pages.description: How to depict the Cartrack software/app itself in photography — device mockups showing real (or realistic) product UI, not placeholder screens.

pages.name: Operations
pages.description: Depot, logistics, and day-to-day operational photography category — warehouses, loading, dispatch.

pages.name: Adjustments and editing
pages.description: Post-processing rules for consistency across a shoot/campaign (see also Colour correcting).

pages.name: Colour correcting
pages.description: Specific colour-grading direction to keep photography consistent with the brand palette (warm/orange-leaning grade implied by examples).

pages.name: What to avoid
pages.description: Explicit exclusions — generic stock photography, staged/inauthentic poses, and imagery inconsistent with the operational tone.

doThis: Use real operational contexts — vehicles on the road, drivers at work, actual depots.
Apply the specified colour-correction grade for consistency across a shoot.
Show the platform/app with realistic (not placeholder) UI when depicting the product.
dontDoThis: Don't use generic corporate stock photography.
Don't stage people in unnatural poses.
Don't mix uncorrected/ungraded imagery into brand material.
relationToProductTokens: No direct token relationship — this section governs marketing photography, not UI. Relevant only if the product ever surfaces marketing imagery in-app (e.g. onboarding, empty states) — in that case, this direction should govern the imagery choice even though it isn't a token.
